checks.finding: design.md ## Architecture and ## Components define the canonical-data/generator/native-module/guard, human-copy, and CI boundaries, describe their interactions and integration with existing guards, and identify JSON, Python, Node, PowerShell 5.1, POSIX shell, embedded C#/.NET P/Invoke, and GitHub Actions technology choices; ## Layer Specifications explicitly records the non-applicable UX/frontend layers and the infrastructure/security refinements.

The dependency graph in design.md ## Architecture is acyclic: canonical data flows to the generator, generated modules flow to runtime guards, generator check output flows to CI enforcement, and the human-copy runner flows to protected live files; no reverse dependency is declared.

design.md ## Data Plan identifies guard-invariants.json as the sole static data entity, names the duplicated guard arrays and regex sources as existing data affected, and specifies the canonical-source/generated-module/human-copy/CI migration strategy with no stored-user-data migration.

design.md ## API / Contract Plan explicitly states that no network endpoint, RPC, webhook, event schema, or browser/service API is added, changed, deprecated, or removed, then defines the repository-local helper, risk-checker, generator, and AnchoredCopySession contracts and their inputs, outputs, and failure behavior.

design.md ## Security Boundaries names all four trust boundaries and records authorization mechanisms, data classifications, and relevant OWASP concerns; security-spec.md further defines B1-B4 validation/auth controls, STRIDE threats, HMAC handling, authorization defaults, data protection, OWASP mapping, and security tests.

SKIP: Feature Type is enforcement-chain security and workflow hardening; ux-spec.md and frontend-spec.md explicitly declare no rendered/browser surface, frontend state, route, component tree, or API client, while design.md declares no network API.

design.md ## Test Strategy covers focused unit/static checks for the HMAC helper, evidence-path helper, risk checker, generator, and copy API; cross-runtime and component-boundary integration fixtures; instrumented fixture-only failure injection; and acceptance verification through TEST-001..013. acceptance-tests.md maps every AC to a named test target, while design.md names concrete evidence including generate-guard-invariants.py --check, reviewed golden fixtures, CI guard suites, exact prefix/suffix digests, rollback digests, and post-install verification.

Every component referenced by design.md ## Architecture and ## API / Contract Plan is defined in ## Components, ### Native module loading contract, ### Human-copy integrity contract, or the local contract bullets; generated modules, runtime guards, risk checker, HMAC and evidence helpers, AnchoredCopySession, human-copy runner, and CI check all have stated responsibilities and technologies.

design.md ## ADR Change Log records the new anchored no-follow protected-copy architecture as accepted ADR 0011 at the canonical docs/adr/0011-phase2-handle-relative-protected-copy.md path; no additional unrecorded architectural departure is introduced.
Applicability skipped: design.md ## Design System Compliance records N/A - ds_profile: none, and ux-spec.md confirms that no rendered interface, component, or design token is consumed.

Applicability skipped: requirements.md contains no Bounded-Context field or approved-domain aggregate references, and the design introduces repository-local configuration and enforcement tooling rather than domain entities.
